changes_made: Changed k8s_ingress_tls_enabled to k8s_ingress_tls_enforced for clarity
review_notes: The functions map well to Kubernetes security controls related to routing traffic through authenticated proxies. The naming conventions were mostly followed, but slight adjustments were made for clarity and specificity. The use of NetworkPolicies, TLS for Ingress, and service mesh mTLS are appropriate mappings for this control.
naming_improvements: Use 'enforced' instead of 'enabled' for TLS to indicate a mandatory requirement
Use 'required' instead of 'enabled' for authentication to specify necessity
title: Boundary Protection | Route Traffic to Authenticated Proxy Servers
step1_reasoning: The control SC-7 (8) requires routing traffic through authenticated proxy servers, which can be mapped to Kubernetes by ensuring that ingress traffic is controlled and authenticated. This can be achieved through NetworkPolicies that deny all ingress by default, enabling TLS for Ingress resources to ensure encrypted communication, requiring authentication on ingress controllers, and using service mesh features like mutual TLS (mTLS) for traffic encryption and authentication within the cluster.
